title: Agenomic System (v0.2)
description: Declarative description of a multi-agent system: the member agents, the orchestration topology that connects them, the shared state and signals they exchange, and the system-wide autonomy, guardrail, and escalation envelope (RFC 0009). The manifest describes design, not execution state.

spec_version.description: Pins the Agenomic specification version this system targets.

id.description: Stable system identifier of the form system://<org>/<name>.

domain.description: Free-form domain tag (e.g. claims, support, trading-risk).

version.description: Version of the system definition itself (semver recommended).

agents.description: Member agents. Each member binds a system-local role name to an agent identity and optionally to its genome bundle.

orchestration.description: How the member agents are composed.

style.description: pipeline: linear hand-offs; graph: explicit edges with optional conditions; supervisor: one role routes to the others; swarm: peer-to-peer hand-offs without a fixed topology; custom: none of the above (describe in `description`).

engine.description: Optional, non-normative hint about the runtime executing the orchestration (e.g. temporal, langgraph, crewai, custom). Conformant tooling MUST NOT require it.

entrypoint.description: Role that receives the initial input.

edges.description: `graph` and `pipeline` styles: directed hand-offs between roles. `END` is a reserved terminal vertex.

shared_state.description: Reference to the schema of the state shared between member agents. Only the schema is referenced; runtime state is NEVER bundled (RFC 0006 applies).

schema_path.description: Bundle-relative path to the shared state schema artifact.

signals.description: External signals the system can receive while running (e.g. documents_received, expert_report_received, human_decision_applied).

workflows.description: Workflow manifests owned by this system (validated by workflow.schema.json).

path.description: Bundle-relative path to the workflow manifest.

communication_guardrails.description: Named output guardrails every member's external communication must respect (e.g. formal_address, no_payment_commitment, no_refusal_wording_without_human_review).

escalation_rules.description: System-wide escalation table: when `condition` holds, the case is routed to `route` (a human role, team, or queue).

forbidden_autonomy.description: Actions no member agent may ever take autonomously, regardless of per-member autonomy declarations.

role.description: System-local role name, unique within the system (e.g. intake, coverage, triage).

genome.description: Optional path or URI to the member's genome bundle. When present, tooling SHOULD resolve and cross-validate it.

autonomy.description: Per-member autonomy envelope inside this system.

kind.description: Runtime family, e.g. temporal, langgraph, crewai, autogen, custom.

to.description: Target role, or the reserved terminal vertex END.

when.description: Optional guard expression; the hand-off happens only when it holds.

payload_schema_path.description: Optional bundle-relative path to the signal payload schema.

condition.description: Free-form condition tag or expression (e.g. bodily_injury, suspected_fraud).

route.description: Human role, team, or queue that receives the escalation.
